question_text: Suppose that 𝑥 is an observed sample of size 1 from a population with probability density function 𝑓(⋅). Based on 𝑥, consider testing 𝐻0: 𝑓(𝑦) = √2𝜋 𝑒− 𝑦2 2 ; 𝑦∈ℝ against 𝐻1: 𝑓(𝑦) = 1 2 𝑒−|𝑦| ; 𝑦∈ℝ. Then which one of the following statements is true?

options.A: The most powerful test rejects 𝐻0 if |𝑥| > 𝑐 for some 𝑐> 0
options.B: The most powerful test rejects 𝐻0 if |𝑥| < 𝑐 for some 𝑐> 0
options.C: The most powerful test rejects 𝐻0 if ||𝑥| −1| > 𝑐 for some 𝑐> 0
options.D: The most powerful test rejects 𝐻0 if ||𝑥| −1| < 𝑐 for some 𝑐> 0
